slides.title: Action and Movement
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 4 English, Unit 5: Action and Movement. Welcome to Unit 5! This unit is all about action and movement — how people and animals move, how we describe fast and slow actions, and how we make good choices when we feel excited or under pressure. You will read about a runner who learns to accelerate, a horse that gallops across a field, and children who rescue a lost animal. You will meet strong verbs like squeeze, peek, proceed, and defend, and you will learn to say exactly *how* an action happens — quickly, slowly, or carefully. By the end, you will be ready to enjoy this unit's adventure story, “The Spiral Cave”. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Use strong action verbs to describe how people and animals move
Tell a story about the past using the past simple tense (played, galloped, rescued, went)
Describe actions with adverbs of manner (quickly, slowly, carefully)
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to use strong action verbs to describe how people and animals move. you will also tell a story about the past using the past simple tense (played, galloped, rescued, went). you will also describe actions with adverbs of manner (quickly, slowly, carefully). Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: gallop, accelerate, proceed, spiral, rate, peek. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
bullets: The Race at the Village Field
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes The Race at the Village Field and How Animals Move. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Past Simple Tense
bullets: The past simple tense is used to talk about actions that already happened and are finished. It lets us tell stories about the past — what we did yesterday...
slides.narration: The first language focus is Past Simple Tense. The past simple tense is used to talk about actions that already happened and are finished. It lets us tell stories about the past — what we did yesterday or last week. For example: “She rescued the bird yesterday.” “They galloped across the field.” Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Writing 1: How-To Guide: How to Rescue a Lost Animal
Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Describe an Action. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
